ip_outcome_note: Card name 'Broly's Ring' and the depicted duo of a larger looming figure behind a smaller foreground figure wearing a gold control crown match the canonical Movie 8 scene: Paragus (father) controls Broly (son) via a mind-control headband. The card's green color and Field mechanic (shielding the Broly-controller's hand from the Leader's discard effect) mechanically re-enacts the control relationship. Vision's 'domination and submission' read is consistent. Verified: Broly and Paragus (Dragon Ball Z, Movie 8).

trivia_bullets.text: In Dragon Ball Z: Broly - The Legendary Super Saiyan (1993), Paragus uses a device on his bracelet synced to a mind-control crown on Broly's head to suppress the Legendary Super Saiyan. Broly ultimately shatters the crown and transforms, turning on his father. This card's name and control-relationship art directly depict that plot mechanic [Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly - The Legendary Super Saiyan].
sources: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly – The Legendary Super Saiyan

trivia_bullets.text: BT1-081 is a green [Field] Extra card (1 green energy cost, Common). Its [Permanent] skill prevents the Broly Leader's forced-discard from affecting your hand — mechanically mirroring Paragus holding Broly's power in check. It is considered a must-include in any BT1 Broly archetype deck [Bandai DBS Strategy: Galactic Battle Deck Breakdowns; WebSearch: retailers/pricecharting].
sources: Bandai DBS Card Game Strategy: Deck Breakdowns Series1 - Broly
WebSearch: pinkbunnygames, bigorbitcards retailer listings BT1-081

trivia_bullets.text: The ring/crown as control artifact has a dual valence in Movie 8 lore: it is simultaneously a leash (Paragus suppressing the monster he created) and a liability (the moment it shatters, Paragus loses control entirely). This is the central dramatic tension of the film's second act [Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly - The Legendary Super Saiyan].
sources: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly – The Legendary Super Saiyan

trivia_bullets.text: Paragus, the controlling father figure depicted behind Broly on this card, is a Saiyan survivor who escaped King Vegeta's execution order alongside infant Broly. He manipulated the New Planet Vegeta scheme to get revenge on King Vegeta's son, using Broly as the instrument — making the control-ring both a safety device and a weapon [Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly - The Legendary Super Saiyan].
sources: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball Z: Broly – The Legendary Super Saiyan
